```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Memory leak from never-cleaned-up CourierTracker instances on WebSocket disconnect, plus unguarded JSON.parse will crash the process on malformed messages."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Memory leak: no WebSocket 'close' handler — CourierTracker instances accumulate forever",
      "risk": "Each courier disconnect leaves a CourierTracker (and its WebSocket reference) in memory. With 5-minute average sessions, thousands of orphaned instances accumulate daily. After 3 months this is the direct cause of the steadily increasing memory usage.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners()",
      "reproduction": "1. Connect 100 couriers. 2. Disconnect all 100. 3. Repeat 10 times. 4. Take a heap snapshot — expect ~1000 CourierTracker instances retained, none GC'd, because the 'message' and 'error' listeners prevent collection.",
      "fix": "Add a 'close' listener that removes all event listeners and nulls references: this.ws.on('close', () => { this.ws.removeAllListeners(); this.ws = null; }). If CourierTracker instances are stored in a Map/Set externally, also delete from that collection. Consider a WeakRef or explicit destroy() method.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Service stability — memory usage must remain bounded under sustained courier churn."
    },
    {
      "title": "Unguarded JSON.parse crashes the entire process on any malformed WebSocket message",
      "risk": "A single malformed message (binary frame, partial UTF-8, truncated JSON) throws a SyntaxError inside the 'message' listener. Since there is no try/catch and no process-level uncaughtException handler shown, this crashes the Node process, disconnecting ALL couriers.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners() — ws.on('message') callback",
      "reproduction": "Send a non-JSON string (e.g. 'ping' or empty string) over the WebSocket. JSON.parse throws, unhandled exception propagates.",
      "fix": "Wrap in try/catch: try { const location = JSON.parse(data); /* validate */ this.updateLocation(location); } catch (e) { console.error('Invalid message', e); }",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Service availability — one bad message must not take down all courier connections."
    },
    {
      "title": "No validation of lat/lng allows corrupt data to persist to database",
      "risk": "Any payload that parses as JSON is forwarded directly to the database. Values like {lat: 999, lng: -999}, {lat: null, lng: undefined}, or {lat: 'DROP TABLE'} are written without checks. Downstream consumers (maps, ETAs, geofencing) silently produce wrong results.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "CourierTracker.updateLocation()",
      "reproduction": "Send {\"lat\": \"hello\", \"lng\": null} over WebSocket. updateLocation writes it to DB. Query the courier's location — get garbage coordinates.",
      "fix": "Validate before writing: check typeof lat === 'number' && typeof lng === 'number' && lat >= -90 && lat <= 90 && lng >= -180 && lng <= 180. Reject and log invalid payloads.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data integrity — courier positions must be valid WGS84 coordinates."
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "db.updateLocation return value is fire-and-forget — silent data loss on DB failure",
      "description": "If db.couriers.updateLocation returns a Promise (likely for any real DB driver), the result is neither awaited nor .catch()'d. A DB outage silently drops all location updates with no error logging and no retry.",
      "location": "CourierTracker.updateLocation()"
    },
    {
      "title": "No rate limiting on location updates — courier can flood DB writes",
      "description": "A misbehaving client or GPS polling at high frequency (e.g. 10Hz) will fire one DB write per message. With thousands of couriers, this can saturate DB connection pool. Consider throttling/debouncing to at most 1 update per second.",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners() — ws.on('message')"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Add a heartbeat/ping-pong mechanism to detect stale connections proactively rather than waiting for TCP timeout.",
    "Consider batching location writes (e.g. buffer in memory, flush every N seconds) to reduce DB pressure.",
    "Log courier disconnect events with session duration for operational visibility."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The memory leak is already in production and worsening. Deploying a fix requires a rolling restart which will momentarily disconnect couriers — coordinate with ops.",
    "rollback_capability": "Fix is additive (adding close handler, try/catch, validation). Safe to roll back — rollback just restores the existing leaky behavior.",
    "backward_compatibility": "No breaking changes to external interfaces. Rejecting invalid coordinates could surface previously-hidden client bugs.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring evident. Add: process.memoryUsage() metrics, active CourierTracker count gauge, invalid-message counter, DB write error rate.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert commit and restart. Existing behavior is broken but stable (slow leak). The fix is low-risk so rollback is unlikely to be needed."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Memory leak diagnosis — classic missing 'close' handler pattern, directly explains the 3-month memory growth symptom",
      "JSON.parse crash — deterministic, trivially reproducible",
      "Missing coordinate validation — no guard exists anywhere in the shown code"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether db.couriers.updateLocation is async (assumed likely but not confirmed)",
      "Whether an external registry holds CourierTracker references (would make leak worse)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "No external process-level uncaughtException handler catches the JSON.parse throw",
      "CourierTracker instances are created per-connection with no external cleanup mechanism",
      "db.couriers.updateLocation is likely async given it's a database call"
    ]
  }
}
```

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code for production risks.

Key hypotheses:
1. Memory leak from CourierTracker instances not being cleaned up on disconnect
2. No JSON.parse error handling - malformed messages crash the handler
3. No WebSocket close listener - resources never freed
4. No coordinate validation
5. Unhandled promise if db.couriers.updateLocation is async

The big one is #1/#3 - memory leak. No `close` event listener means when couriers disconnect, the CourierTracker instance and its WebSocket reference persist. With 5-minute average sessions and frequent reconnects over 3 months, this is the classic memory leak pattern.
